channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Putin opens his annual news conference, an event he uses to reinforce his authority
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin boasted about the economy and hailed his troops' gains in Ukraine during his annual news conference and call-in show Thursday, an event he uses to reinforce his authority and demonstrate sweeping control of the country’s political scene.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Man accused in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing expected to appear at hearing on extradition to New York
item.description: HOLLIDAYSBURG, Pa. (AP) — The suspect charged with shooting to death a health insurance company chief executive on a Manhattan street will be taken Thursday morning to hearings on related Pennsylvania criminal charges and efforts to extradite him to New York.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Columbus serves trespassing notices at dozens of homeless camps
item.description: The city of Columbus, Ohio, is forcing several homeless camps to move out, but activists said they are worried these people will have nowhere to go if the trespass notices keep coming.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Cleary, NewsNation

item.title: Japanese newspaper boss who influenced the nation's postwar politics died at 98
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Tsuneo Watanabe, the powerful head of Japan's largest newspaper who had close ties with the country's powerful conservative leaders, has died, his company said Thursday. He was 98.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Oklahoma prepares to perform the nation's 25th and final execution of 2024
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Oklahoma is preparing to execute a man who killed a 10-year-old girl in what would be the nation's 25th and final execution of the year. Kevin Ray Underwood is scheduled to die by lethal injection on Thursday, his 45th birthday, at the Oklahoma State Penitentiary in McAlester.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How New York prosecutors used a terrorism law in the charges against Luigi Mangione
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York prosecutors are using a 9/11-era anti-terrorism law in their case against the man charged with gunning down UnitedHealthcare's CEO outside a midtown Manhattan hotel.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California man who messaged Wisconsin shooter, planned own attack ordered to surrender guns
item.description: The shooter who killed a student and teacher at a religious school in Wisconsin brought two guns to the school and was in contact with a man in California who authorities say was planning to attack a government building, according to authorities and court documents that became public Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Medical examiner identifies victims killed in Wisconsin Christian school shooting
item.description: Officials have released new details about the shooting at a Madison, Wisconsin school that claimed the life of a student and teacher, and wounded six others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US effort to curb China's and Russia's access to advanced computer chips 'inadequate,' report finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Commerce Department's efforts to curb China's and Russia's access to American-made advanced computer chips have been “inadequate” and will need more funding to stymie their ability to manufacture advanced weapons, according to a report published Wednesday by the Senate's Perman
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What is 'coffee badging?' Workplace trend seen as protest to return-to-office push
item.description: As the post-pandemic return-to-office push gathers steam, again, workers are quietly mounting a pushback of their own.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, Fox 5

item.title: Trump, Vance call for clean CR, debt ceiling hike, urge GOP to sink Johnson compromise
item.description: President-elect Trump and Vice President-elect Vance on Wednesday called for Republicans to approve a clean stopgap funding bill paired with a hike to the debt ceiling, saying the previous government-funding measure negotiated by Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) should be torpedoed.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Chicago White Sox stadium getting a new name for the 2025 season
item.description: The home stadium of the Chicago White Sox won't be called Guaranteed Rate Field come the start of the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NCAA president says there are 'less than 10' transgender athletes in college sports
item.description: NCAA President Charlie Baker told a Senate panel that there are fewer than 10 transgender athletes he is aware of who currently compete in college sports, pouring cold water on an issue Republicans have said is a nationwide problem and one that is increasingly fraught territory for Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KRON4

item.title: New charges target ex-Miami congressman for lobbying on behalf of sanctioned Venezuelan tycoon
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — A federal grand jury in Washington has returned an indictment against former Congressman David Rivera for failing to register as a foreign agent of a sanctioned Venezuelan media tycoon on whose behalf the Miami Republican allegedly lobbied the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rand Paul blocks bill to authorize local, state authorities to track drones
item.description: Sen. Rand Paul (R-Ky.) on Wednesday blocked an effort by Senate Democrats to pass legislation to authorize and provide resources to state and local authorities to track mysterious drones that have been sighted over New Jersey, New York and other states.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some Walmarts testing body cameras for employees
item.description: "We are always looking at new and innovative technology used across the retail industry,” a Walmart spokesperson said.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, NewsNation

item.title: Charlotte Hornets apologize after PS5 taken from 13-year-old during on-court giveaway
item.description: What was supposed to be a festive "skit" during the Charlotte Hornets game against the Philadelphia 76ers on Monday ended up earning the Hornets ‘Grinch’-like jeers on social media.
item.dc:creator: Ciara Lankford, KRON4

item.title: 184-foot floating Christmas tree collapses, killing 21-year-old man
item.description: A 21-year-old man was killed when a 184-foot floating Christmas tree collapsed in Brazil earlier this week. The incident occurred on Monday in the city of Maricá, which is located roughly 25 miles east of Rio de Janeiro.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Wisconsin school shooter had 2 guns and got messages from man accused of plotting his own attack
item.description: Police say the shooter who killed a student and teacher at a religious school in Wisconsin this week brought two guns to the school.
item.dc:creator: Todd Richmond, Scott Bauer And Ryan J. Foley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Students assaulting educators on the rise across Texas
item.description: The number of school employees being assaulted on the job is rising in Texas. More than 2,600 assaults on educators were reported last school year, according to data from the Texas Education Agency.
item.dc:creator: Nina Banks, NewsNation

item.title: Louisiana patient has first severe bird flu case in US: health officials
item.description: Human cases of bird flu have been reported in the U.S. before, but infections were mild in those patients.
item.dc:creator: Trinity Velazquez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Paxton files motion to block auction of border wall materials
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton filed a motion Tuesday to stop the sale of pieces of the border wall.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Jones, NewsNation

item.title: First severe bird flu case confirmed in US: CDC
item.description: Human cases of bird flu have been reported in the U.S. before, but infections were mild in those patients.
item.dc:creator: Trinity Velazquez, WGN 9

item.title: GOP report recommends Liz Cheney be criminally investigated over Jan. 6 work
item.description: It’s a claim that if pursued would likely face significant roadblocks but that nonetheless comes as Trump has suggested members of the Jan. 6 panel should “go to jail” for their work.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Amazon has up to 20% off gift cards ahead of the holidays
item.description: You can save on a wide range of gift cards, making it easier and more affordable to give the gift of shopping and saving.
item.dc:creator: Christina Marfice, KTLA 5

item.title: Biathlon official apologizes to athletes who have suffered mistreatment
item.description: The secretary general of the International Biathlon Union apologized to any athlete who was mistreated during his tenure at U.S. Biathlon while speaking to a 90-minute meeting Tuesday that focused on concerns about athlete safety brought to light by multiple Associated Press reports.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FTC bans junk ticket, hotel fees
item.description: The final rule will become effective 120 days after its publication in the Federal Register, coinciding with the first few months of Trump’s administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: Oscar Mayer accepting applications for the newest class of Wienermobile drivers
item.description: Oscar Mayer is now accepting applications for its coveted “Hotdogger” position, offering recent college graduates the chance to drive the brand’s iconic 27-foot Wienermobile across the country.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Shock poll: 41 percent of young voters find killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO acceptable
item.description: Anger over health insurance companies has been in the spotlight after Thompson was fatally shot Dec. 4 in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: How to claim a 2nd payment from the Equifax data breach settlement
item.description: Equifax has started alerting people of more money waiting for them from a massive fund the credit reporting agency paid to settle a data breach.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Montana judge blocks rule that prevented transgender people from changing their sex on documents
item.description: HELENA, Mont. (AP) — A state judge in Montana has temporarily blocked policies that prevented transgender people from changing the sex designation on their birth certificates and driver’s licenses.
item.dc:creator: AMY BETH HANSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Event promoters, hotels and lodging sites soon will have to disclose extra fees up front
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission on Tuesday said it will soon require hotels, vacation rental platforms and live event promoters to disclose any fees up front when they list prices.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Taco Bell adding chicken nuggets to menus nationwide
item.description: Taco Bell, known for its Mexican-inspired cuisine, will begin serving its take on chicken nuggets later this month. Taco Bell’s Crispy Chicken Nuggets features tortilla breading and will be served with three sauces, including the first-ever Hidden Valley Fire Ranch.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: A timeline of the investigation of the Gilgo Beach killings
item.description: For years, women had been disappearing on Long Island. Then in 2010, investigators searching for a missing woman began finding 10 sets of human remains in the scrub along a barrier island parkway, not far from the sands of New York's remote Gilgo Beach.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What we know about the 2 killed, 6 injured in Wisconsin Christian school shooting
item.description: A student and teacher were killed and six others — five students and a teacher — were injured in Monday's shooting.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Suspect in Gilgo Beach serial killings is charged in the death of a seventh woman
item.description: The New York architect facing murder charges in a string of deaths known as the Gilgo Beach killings was charged on Tuesday in the death of a seventh woman.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press via Nexstar Media Wire, WGN 9

item.title: Trump tariff plan threatens to drive up gas prices
item.description: Trump said that on his first day in office, he would place 25 percent tariffs on all Canadian and Mexican goods. The two countries are the leading sources of U.S. oil imports.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WFLA, NBC
